hero.subtitle: AI tailored to Turkey's climate, built for mid-sized greenhouse operations.

problem.description: Technology is expensive, no local solutions exist, the market is unreachable. This gap is an 800,000-hectare opportunity.

water.label: Water Wasted
water.description: Annual water loss in Turkey's agriculture sector. Most of it avoidable with smart irrigation.

cost.label: Current System Cost
cost.description: Installation cost of premium systems like Netafim. Out of reach for mid-scale Turkish growers.

market.label: Hectares Under Cover
market.description: Turkey's untapped greenhouse market. AgroNext targets this segment.

product.description: Four integrated layers, a single mobile app. Install, connect, profit.

hardware.title: Field Hardware
hardware.detail: Humidity, temperature, soil-moisture and light sensors. ESP32 microcontroller operates off-grid. Setup: 4 hours.

ai.title: AI Engine
ai.detail: GRU model trained on Turkish climate data. Target prediction accuracy: 94%+.

cloud.title: Cloud Infrastructure
cloud.detail: Neon Postgres, Vercel serverless and event-driven processing. Low cost, scalable.

app.title: Mobile App
app.detail: AI alerts, scenario-based automation and one-tap action buttons.

phone.alert: AI Alert: Soil moisture in Greenhouse 2 is approaching critical level. Irrigation recommended.

one.title: Field Hardware
one.subtitle: Edge Layer
one.description: ESP32-based sensor network measuring soil moisture, temperature, humidity, light and CO₂. Solar-powered and grid-independent.

two.title: Artificial Intelligence
two.subtitle: GRU Model
two.description: GRU-based deep learning model processing time-series data. Unlike threshold systems, it generates proactive predictions.

three.title: Cloud Infrastructure
three.subtitle: Cloud Layer
three.description: Sensor data, AI inferences and notifications processed on open-source cloud infrastructure. Low cost, high scalability.

four.title: User Application
four.subtitle: Mobile & Web
four.description: Growers remotely monitor and control the greenhouse via mobile and web, receiving instant alerts on critical thresholds.

about.title: Who we are
about.subtitle: Founded to make technology accessible to every Turkish grower.

mission.title: Why AgroNext?
mission.lead: Mid-sized greenhouse operations in Turkey can't access digital agriculture technology because of the high cost of smart greenhouse systems like Netafim.

one.label: Setup cost — out of reach for mid-scale growers.

two.label: Annual water waste — preventable, yet unprevented.

solution.title: Our solution
solution.body:
AgroNext delivers a GRU-based AI tuned for Turkey's climate, a solar-powered sensor network and a subscription model — offering an accessible smart greenhouse system at one-tenth the price of global rivals.

Unlike threshold-based systems, it generates proactive predictions: growers see irrigation, ventilation and fertilization needs ahead of time, cutting resource waste.

story.title: Our story
paragraphs: AgroNext was born from the shared idea of three high-school students and an academic. Seeing firsthand the technology gap for Turkish growers, the team set out to build a local, accessible alternative to global rivals.

The venture is developed with the academic guidance of Gebze Technical University. Training the AI model on Turkish climate data and shaping field-testing methodology both happen through this university partnership.

From hardware to AI, from cloud infrastructure to the mobile app — every layer is built in-house, from scratch. The goal: lead the digital transformation of Turkey's 50,000+ mid-scale greenhouses through a path that is truly attainable.
Today we're at the MVP stage, moving towards our first pilot greenhouses and commercialization.
